mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.50% and remains there for at least one month, the zero lower bound constraint becomes binding. This signals exhausted conventional monetary policy transmission, reducing the equity risk premium compression channel and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors reassess growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS=0.15% indicates zero lower bound regime
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.50%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window in ZLB periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals deteriorating labor market momentum. This triggers a forward-looking reassessment of consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances following PAYEMS month-over-month declines, or correlation between PAYEMS change and 5-day SPX return is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in real retail sales (RSAFS) over two consecutive months signals weakening consumer demand and reduced near-term corporate revenue growth. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity valuations compress in response to lower earnings expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 58% of instances following 2-month RSAFS declines, or Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS momentum and 20-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 0.5% month-over-month while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) remains elevated above 8.0%. This signals supply-side expansion without full labor market tightening, indicating sustainable earnings growth without wage-driven inflation, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with slack labor market
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns following trigger are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) when both conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) rise above 550 thousand units annualized while real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains positive. This joint signal indicates sustained residential investment and broad-based demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the market prices in multi-quarter earnings growth.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 550 thousand units AND GDPC1 growth > 0% YoY
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) when both conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 3.0% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 2.0%, a monetary policy lag emerges. This signals real rates are negative and policy is accommodative despite inflation, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors seek inflation hedges in equities.
regime_hint: negative real rates with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) when both conditions are met, or correlation between real rate proxy and 20-day returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.05) over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, signaling accelerating labor market deterioration. This triggers a forward-looking recession signal that produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as equity investors reprice tail risk and earnings expectations downward.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration and recession risk
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 62% of instances following UNRATE increases > 0.3pp over 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is below 5.0%, signaling a supply shock in a tight labor market. This produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors anticipate stagflationary pressures and margin compression.
regime_hint: supply shock with tight labor market
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) when both conditions are met, or Rank IC > -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window in this regime.
